raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's core thesis, contribution framing, and whether the stated deliverable matches the claimed method contribution.","R1.2":"The artifact contains argument-chain issues, especially around claiming a fully unsupervised detector while using human-labeled calibration data for threshold selection.","R1.3":"Assessing whether the reported experiments support the claims is central because the method, guarantees, benchmark, and empirical results are all used to justify strong contribution claims.","R1.4":"The text makes explicit first-work and no-prior-work claims, so novelty relative to nearby work is a central expected responsibility.","R1.5":"The narrative shifts from detector and guarantees to the benchmark as the principal deliverable, making section-level contribution organization relevant.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation criteria.","RX.2":"The artifact includes strong claims such as first, formal guarantees, and fully unsupervised, making overclaim avoidance active.","RX.3":"The agent must only project responsibilities and not perform the requested evaluation of the artifact.","RX.4":"Potential downstream harm from overstated benchmark or bias-detection claims makes caution about misuse and unsupported conclusions active.","RX.5":"Traceability is required so each responsibility assignment is grounded in specific features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's core thesis, contribution framing, and whether the stated deliverable matches the claimed method contribution.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains argument-chain issues, especially around claiming a fully unsupervised detector while using human-labeled calibration data for threshold selection.
rationale.R1.3: Assessing whether the reported experiments support the claims is central because the method, guarantees, benchmark, and empirical results are all used to justify strong contribution claims.
rationale.R1.4: The text makes explicit first-work and no-prior-work claims, so novelty relative to nearby work is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: The narrative shifts from detector and guarantees to the benchmark as the principal deliverable, making section-level contribution organization relevant.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation criteria.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact includes strong claims such as first, formal guarantees, and fully unsupervised, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not perform the requested evaluation of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harm from overstated benchmark or bias-detection claims makes caution about misuse and unsupported conclusions active.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is required so each responsibility assignment is grounded in specific features of the delegation and artifact.
